title: Manage a Campaign Dial List
lede: The dial list is the queue of leads and contacts a campaign will call. Learn how to add people, review their attempt status, and clean up the list.

steps.title: Open the campaign
steps.content:
1. Go to Campaigns in the left menu.
  2. Click a campaign's name to open it.
  3. Inside, use the tabs at the top: Overview, Settings, Dial List, Add Leads, Add Contacts, and Stages.

images.caption: The tabs inside a campaign, including Dial List, Add Leads, and Add Contacts
The tabs inside a campaign, including Dial List, Add Leads, and Add Contacts

steps.title: Add leads and contacts
steps.content:
1. Open the Add Leads or Add Contacts tab.
  2. Search your CRM by name, phone, or other fields.
  3. Select the people you want and add them — they are queued into this campaign's dial list.

Tip: keep your CRM lists tidy so it's easy to pull the right audience. New leads live under CRM → Leads.

steps.title: Review the dial list
steps.content:
Open the Dial List tab to see everyone queued. For each row you can see:

  • The contact or lead and their phone number.
  • Attempts made and attempts left.
  • The current lifecycle stage or last outcome.

Use the search and filters to narrow the list by stage or status.

steps.title: Remove or clean up entries
steps.content:
• Select rows and use the bulk Delete action to remove people you no longer want to call.
  • The Overview tab shows live progress: contacts, attempts made, attempts left, and finished counts.

Numbers on your Do Not Call list are automatically skipped — you never have to remove them by hand.

steps.title: What happens when it's done
steps.content: When every contact has been fully attempted, the campaign moves to Completed and its dial list becomes a read-only archive (you'll see an "Archive view" banner). Completed campaigns can't be edited — to run the same audience again, create a fresh campaign and add them to its dial list.
